content.explanation:
🧾 What are Forms?

Until now, your websites only displayed information. But real websites allow users to send information back.

This is done using HTML forms.

🌐 Real-Life Examples

  • Login pages
  • Sign-up forms
  • Search bars
  • Contact forms

All of these are built using HTML forms.

⚡ Core Idea

A form collects user input like text, email, or passwords and sends it to a server for processing.

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Basic Form Structure

<form>
  <input type="text" placeholder="Enter your name">
  <input type="email" placeholder="Enter your email">
  <button>Submit</button>
</form>

📌 Important Elements

  • <form> → container for inputs
  • <input> → user text fields
  • <button> → submits form

🧠 Key Rule

👉 Forms are used to collect data, not just display content

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
Contact Me

Send me a message using the form below:

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Build your first form

1. Add a <form> under the paragraph
2. Inside it add:
   - Text input for name
   - Email input
   - A submit button

3. Run your code and test the layout

🔥 This is your first interactive web feature
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Example:
<form>
  <input type="text">
</form>

Remember: inputs go inside form

quiz_engine.question: What is the main purpose of an HTML form?
options: To collect user input and send data
To display images on a webpage
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 Forms are used to collect user input like names, emails, and messages that can be sent to a server.

takeaways: Forms allow users to send data to websites
Forms are the foundation of login and signup systems
